title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Understanding the (0) RELATIONSHIP (RELATION) between diet and health is crucial for a long life. Nutritional science has made (17) REMARKABLE (REMARK) progress in identifying the vitamins we need. A (18) BALANCED (BALANCE) diet provides the body with enough energy to function (19) EFFICIENTLY (EFFECT). However, the (20) CONSUMPTION (CONSUME) of processed foods high in sugar has led to an (21) ALARMING (ALARM) increase in obesity rates. Health (22) PROFESSIONALS (PROFESSION) recommend eating more whole grains and fresh vegetables. This (23) ADVICE (ADVISE) is often ignored due to the (24) CONVENIENCE (CONVENIENT) of fast food options in modern cities. To improve public health, (25) EDUCATIONAL (EDUCATION) programs must be implemented to teach children about healthy eating habits. By fostering a culture that values nutrition, we can reduce the burden of chronic diseases and ensure that future generations have the tools they need to lead active and fulfilling lives in an increasingly health-conscious world.

example.text: Understanding the (0) RELATIONSHIP (RELATION) between diet and health...
example.explanation: Noun required to describe a connection; RELATION → RELATIONSHIP.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'progress'; REMARK → REMARKABLE.
Adjective required to describe 'diet'; BALANCE → BALANCED.
Adverb required to modify the verb 'function'; EFFECT → EFFICIENTLY.
Adjective required to modify 'increase'; ALARM → ALARMING.
Plural noun for people required; PROFESSION → PROFESSIONALS.
Noun required after the demonstrative 'this'; ADVISE → ADVICE.
Noun required after the definite article; CONVENIENT → CONVENIENCE.
